steps.title: Follow the cause and result
steps.content: The reed absorbs chromium from polluted soil and stores it in its roots and shoots. The next clause describes the result of that absorption: chromium levels in the soil go down.

steps.title: Use the result transition
steps.content: A would introduce a more exact detail, B would signal contrast, and D would merely add a separate point. The sentence needs a transition showing that decreased soil chromium follows from the plant's absorption. The answer is C.
